task.name: Spec Sync Quick Check
task.description: Lightweight drift check for git hook integration. Returns CLEAN or DRIFT_DETECTED summary. Use for automated pre-commit or pre-push validation.
task.objective: Quick check if recent code changes have drifted from specifications, without full analysis

input.name: changed_files
input.desc: List of files changed in the commit (from git diff --name-only)

llm.mandate: This is a QUICK check - do NOT perform full drift analysis
Focus only on obvious mismatches between changed files and spec documents
Must complete in under 30 seconds - no deep analysis

step.title: Categorize Changed Files
step.action: Classify each changed file into categories:
- API routes / endpoints → check against architecture API section
- Database models / migrations → check against architecture data model
- Package dependencies → check against architecture technology constraints
- Configuration files → check against architecture deployment section
- UI components → check against UX design (if available)

step.title: Quick Drift Scan
step.action: For each categorized file, do a lightweight check:
- Are new API routes reflected in architecture API specs?
- Are new dependencies in the approved technology list?
- Are database changes reflected in the data model?
- Are new UI components mentioned in UX design?
Note: This is pattern-matching, not deep semantic analysis

check.action: Output: CLEAN - No obvious spec drift detected in changed files
Output: DRIFT_DETECTED - [brief description of potential drift areas]
Suggest: Run full Spec-Code Sync (SC) for detailed analysis
